name: Hualien County (Taiwan)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace, and a bright future
the Pacific Ocean that forms the eastern coast and the sky over the Central Mountain Range
the forests of the Central Mountain Range
the warm sun and the riches of the eastern coast
the warmth of the indigenous and Han people of the county
the cultural heritage of the Truku, Amis and other indigenous peoples
the marble and stone of the Taroko Gorge

description: A white field charged at the centre with a richly detailed multicolour emblem combining stylised flowers, sea and mountain motifs, with horizontal coloured bands at the bottom of the flag and the wordmark 'Hualien County' below.
history: Hualien County (Hualian Xian) occupies a long stretch of Taiwan's east coast, between the Central Mountain Range and the Pacific Ocean. The current county flag bears an elaborate multi-element emblem representing the county's six main ethnic communities (the Han Chinese and five indigenous peoples — Amis, Truku, Sakizaya, Kavalan and Bunun), the Taroko Gorge, the coast, and the surrounding mountains, together with horizontal stripes representing the county's six districts.

features.role: Hualien County emblem with multi-colour floral and landscape motifs
features.symbolism: elaborate emblem combining flowers (representing the six main communities of the county), abstract mountains and waves, the Taroko Gorge, and the eastern Pacific; expresses Hualien's natural beauty and cultural diversity

features.role: stripes representing the districts and communities of Hualien
features.symbolism: six narrow horizontal stripes at the foot of the flag, representing the diverse communities and districts of Hualien County
